_comment: Known-good AWS SNS test vector. The canonical string was built per AWS SNS signing spec (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sns-verify-signature-of-message.html) and signed with a one-off RSA-2048 keypair whose PRIVATE KEY was discarded on purpose — only the public key + signature are committed here. This makes the fixture offline-reproducible while removing the private-key exfiltration risk. To regenerate: openssl genrsa / dgst -sha256 -sign, then drop privateKeyPem before committing. SnsSignatureValidationTests loads this file, feeds the public key into the ISnsSigningCertificateCache mock (no network), and asserts that SnsPayloadSignatureValidator returns Valid.

signingKeys._comment: SPKI public key used to verify the signature above. The matching private key was destroyed on fixture creation and cannot be recovered — rotating the fixture requires a fresh keypair.
